message:
fix(index): gate CASS_INDEX_WATCH_ONCE_PATHS env var on --watch mode

`read_watch_once_paths_env()` pulls its list from a test-oriented env
variable. The old resolution path took the explicit CLI `watch_once`
argument first and fell back to the env var regardless of whether the
user had actually asked for watch mode:

    watch_once.filter(|p| !p.is_empty()).or_else(read_watch_once_paths_env)

That was fine in isolation but caused a real leak: any environment that
happened to have `CASS_INDEX_WATCH_ONCE_PATHS` set (notably the
integration-test harness, which exports it for the duration of a suite)
would bleed those paths into every `cass index ...` subprocess invoked
from the same env, even the ones that were supposed to do a normal
non-watch one-shot index. End result: tests passed, but a bystander
`cass index` would silently replace its input with a ghost path and
either no-op or index the wrong source.

Add `resolve_watch_once_paths{_from_sources}` helpers with explicit
priority — explicit CLI paths always win; env paths are only consulted
when `watch == true`; otherwise `None`. Replace the direct `.or_else`
at the call site in `run_index_with_data`.

Three unit tests in `watch_once_resolution_tests` pin the contract:
- `normal_index_ignores_test_watch_once_env_without_watch_mode`
- `watch_mode_can_consume_test_watch_once_env_paths`
- `explicit_watch_once_paths_win_even_without_watch_flag`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
